title: palicourea subtomentosa subsp. subtomentosa
intro: Palicourea subtomentosa subsp. subtomentosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the "canela-branca" or "white cinnamon". The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. It is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antiseptic properties. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of ailments.
